memoryArtifacts.description:
BYU Film Number 355556
EGBERT GENEALOGY
Descendants of Egbert Sanderson Collateral Lines A-Z, This genealogy was Collected and organized in 5 volumes by Darrell Dwight Matthews.  Microfilmed May 1963.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Philip Young於1409年出生在英國史洛普爾卡納姆時，他的父親Richard Young, Bishop Young34歲，母親Alice Rossell30歲。他於1429年與Agnes Bonarton在英國英格蘭史洛普爾卡納姆結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1459年9月23日在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾布洛爾享年50歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Philip Young于1409年出生在英国史洛普爾卡納姆时，他的父亲Richard Young, Bishop Young34岁，母亲Alice Rossell30岁。他于1429年与Agnes Bonarton在英国英格兰史洛普爾卡納姆结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1459年9月23日在英国英格兰斯塔福爾布洛爾享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: Young
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish: nickname from Middle English yong ‘young’ (Old English geong), used to distinguish a younger man from an older man bearing the same personal name (typically, father and son). In Middle English this name is often found with the Anglo-Norman French definite article, for example Robert le Yunge. In Gaelic-speaking areas of Scotland this was widely used as an English equivalent of the Gaelic nickname Og ‘young’; see Ogg . This surname is also very common among African Americans.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘young’ or similar, notably German   Jung  , Dutch   Jong   and   De Jong  , and French   Lejeune   and   Lajeunesse  .
Americanized form of Swedish Ljung: topographic or an ornamental name from ljung ‘(field of) heather’, or a habitational name from a placename containing this word, e.g. Ljungby.

variants.name: Yongue
meanings: English: variant of   Young  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.
Perhaps also an Americanized form of Dutch   De Jonge  .

variants.name: Youngs
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of Young , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Younger
meanings: English (mainly Northumberland and eastern Scotland): from Middle English yungre ‘younger’, comparative of Young , used to distinguish a younger man from an older man bearing the same personal name (typically, father and son). In one case, at least, however, the name is known to have been borne by an immigrant Fleming, and was probably an Americanized form of Middle Dutch jongheer ‘young nobleman’ (see Jonker ).
Americanized form of various cognate or like-sounding names in other languages, notably German   Junger   and   Junker  , or Dutch   Jonker  .

variants.name: Ogg
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of a nickname from the Gaelic adjective óg ‘young’, used to distinguish the junior of two bearers of the same personal name (typically, father and son).
English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Hogg  , pronounced without initial /h/.

variants.name: Younge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Fitzroy, Sinead.

English: variant of   Young  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 823, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: Philip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek name Philippos, meaning ‘lover of horses’, from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’. This was popular in the classical period and since. It was the name of the father of Alexander the Great . It was also the name of one of Christ's apostles, of a deacon ordained by the apostles after the death of Christ, and of several other early saints. See also Philippa .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Philip Young was born in 1409, in Caynham, Shropshire, England, his father, Richard Young, Bishop Young, was 34 and his mother, Alice Rossell, was 30. He married Agnes Bonarton in 1429, in Caynham, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 23 September 1459, in Blore, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Philip Young est né en 1409, à Caynham, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Young, Bishop Young, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Alice Rossell, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Bonarton en 1429, à Caynham, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 23 septembre 1459, à Blore, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Philip Young 1409 in Caynham, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Young, Bishop Young 34 und seine Mutter Alice Rossell 30. Er hat 1429 in Caynham, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom Agnes Bonarton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 23. September 1459 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Blore, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Philip Young è nato il nel 1409 nel Caynham, Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Richard Young, Bishop Young, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Alice Rossell, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Agnes Bonarton nel 1429, nel Caynham, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 23 settembre 1459, nel Blore, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Philip Youngが1409年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアケインハムで生まれた時，父Richard Young, Bishop Youngは34歳，母Alice Rossellは30歳であった。1429年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアケインハムでAgnes Bonartonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1459年9月23日にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアブロアで50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Philip Young(이)가 1409년 영국 슈럽실르 카이함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Young, Bishop Young(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Rossell(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1429년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 카이함에서 Agnes Bonarton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1459년 9월 23일 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 블로어에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Philip Young nasceu em 1409, em Caynham, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Young, Bishop Young, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Alice Rossell, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes Bonarton em 1429, em Caynham, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 23 de setembro de 1459, em Blore, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Philip Young родился в 1409 г. в местности кеинам, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Young, Bishop Young было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Alice Rossell, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Bonarton в 1429 г. в местности кеинам, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 23 сентября 1459 г. в местности блор, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 50 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Philip Young nació en 1409, en Caynham, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Young, Bishop Young, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Alice Rossell, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Agnes Bonarton en 1429, en Caynham, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 23 de septiembre de 1459, en Blore, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 50 años.
